scenario: A DMN computes a deadline as applicationDate + duration("P30D") (30 days after application). The team wants to combine this with a time component to produce a date and time value at midnight UTC. Which FEEL constructor combines a date and a time into a date and time value?
question: Which FEEL constructor combines a date and a time into a date and time value?

options.text: The expression date + time uses arithmetic addition between a date value and a time value, but FEEL does not define addition between these two types. FEEL arithmetic for dates and times is specifically defined for adding or subtracting durations to/from dates or date-times, not for composing a date-and-time value from separate date and time components via the + operator. The correct mechanism for combining a separate date and a separate time into a single date-and-time value is the dedicated two-argument constructor function.

md:255-278 (NOT in temporal functions). It combines a separate date value and a separate time value into a single date-and-time result. For the scenario, the expression would be =date and time(applicationDate + duration("P30D"), time("00:00:00")) to produce a date-and-time at midnight UTC on the computed deadline date.

The concat function in FEEL is designed for string concatenation -- it joins two or more string values together into a single combined string. Applying concat to a date value and a time value would either produce nonsensical results or raise a type error, since these are not string types. FEEL provides purpose-built temporal constructor functions for composing date-and-time values from their component parts, which must be used instead.

FEEL does provide a mechanism to combine a separate date value with a separate time value into a single date-and-time result. The date and time(date, time) two-argument constructor form is explicitly documented in the FEEL built-in functions conversion library.

a.text: Incorrect. FEEL has explicit constructors for date / time / date and time / duration. The date and time(date, time) two-argument form combines a date value and a time value into a date-and-time. Used when you have a date from one source and a time from another and need to combine them. Single-argument forms exist too: date and time("2026-06-01T09:00:00Z") parses an ISO 8601 string directly. date("2026-06-01") parses a date string. time("09:00:00") parses a time string. These are constructors from strings. Practical: for "midnight of a computed date," use date and time(computedDate, time("00:00:00")).

b.text: Correct. Date+time arithmetic isn't a defined operation.

explanation: Date+time arithmetic isn't a defined operation.
